raw_text:
WARNING [TC-K-004] interface.f90:5 <-> header.h:2
  LONG_DOUBLE_PORTABILITY: REAL(c_long_double) / 'long double' width is
  platform-defined:
    x86-64 Linux (gfortran): 80-bit extended (16 bytes with 6 padding)
    x86-64 Windows (MSVC):   64-bit double (8 bytes) — C_LONG_DOUBLE = -1!
    Apple ARM64 (clang):      128-bit quad (16 bytes, IEEE 754-2008)
    IBM PowerPC (xlf):        128-bit IBM double-double (16 bytes)
  Interface is correct on Linux but silently wrong width on Windows.
  C_LONG_DOUBLE is -1 on platforms where long double is unsupported.

NOTE: Check iso_c_binding::C_LONG_DOUBLE /= -1 at compile time.
      For portable 128-bit: use REAL(c_float128) if available (GCC extension).
